creator: khwerz@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Alan Somers from comment #4)

My vote is in considering them all as remote.

(In reply to Alan Somers from comment #5)

It is not optimal but good enough, this is beyond me so I don't know if fusefs can be required to somehow announce if it is a remotefs. Given that freebsd has control of the base system and ports, unlike gnu, including and excluding should be easier no?

This is offtopic but I thought I could do a wrapper that if it found -l would run the appropriate command as in -t nofoo,nobar as the manpage states that you add "no" as a prefix, as in:

> /bin/df -Thn -t nofusefs.rclone,nofusefs.sshfs

I thought that to mean to each item, but instead its if you add no it goes into a strange exclusion mode? IDK if the man page should be changed or if it is correct on that regard.
